question: According to the Camunda 8 "Working with APIs & tools" overview, which set of official clients and SDKs is offered to simplify API usage?

options.text: Java and a REST-only client — no Node.js or Spring SDK is shipped.

a.text: The overview page explicitly states that the official Camunda clients and SDKs are Java, Spring, and Node.js, and that these simplify API usage and speed up development against Camunda 8 APIs.

b.text: Python and Go are not listed on the overview page as official Camunda 8 clients/SDKs. The named set is Java, Spring, and Node.js.

c.text: The overview does not name TypeScript or C# in its list of official clients; Node.js (not TypeScript) is the JavaScript-runtime client called out, and there is no C# entry on this page.

d.text: The overview names Spring and Node.js alongside Java as official clients, so saying only Java + REST is offered contradicts the page.

explanation: The "Working with APIs & tools" overview lists Java, Spring, and Node.js as the official Camunda clients and SDKs that simplify API usage and speed up development on top of the Camunda 8 APIs.
